name: BACKPORCH COFFEE (GREENWOOD)
address: 706 NE GREENWOOD AVE SUITE 140, BEND, OR 97701

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Limited Rear Access; Multiple Structures
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness on this property could be achieved by ensuring the single access point remains unobstructed and easily identifiable, which would facilitate responder entry and strategic movement. Additionally, optimizing the layout of the vehicle parking area to prevent congestion may enhance staging capabilities and manoeuvrability for emergency vehicles. Implementing signage or markers that clearly delineate movement paths to and around various structures could support navigation and coordination efforts. Consideration could also be given to establishing a secondary access point or ensuring that the limited rear access is free of obstructions, as this might improve personnel access to all sides of each structure. Maintaining sightlines around the landscaped area would assist responders in swiftly identifying key locations and tactical opportunities within the property.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Limited Defensible Space; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between the structure and adjacent vegetation could improve defensible space and reduce potential fire exposure. Creating more open space around the property by managing this vegetation is advised, as it may limit the intensity and continuity of fuels near the structure. The presence of open terrain may already provide an advantage by limiting fuel concentrations, so preserving and augmenting these areas can further enhance the property’s preparedness. Regular maintenance to clear excess vegetation within the immediate vicinity of the building can ensure better defensible space and potentially improve conditions for firefighter access. Implementing a long-term plan for managing the surrounding landscape could support ongoing wildfire resilience and property protection.
